title: malache glechomifolia
intro: Malache glechomifolia, also known as gale of the woods, is a perennial plant native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Malache glechomifolia include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as a diuretic to relieve urinary tract infections. Malache glechomifolia can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
